jokes.text: Stew has never woken anyone up, ever.
jokes.setup: The gang trying to wake up Dee with stew fumes
jokes.punchline: Charlie's matter-of-fact dismissal of their ridiculous plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - cutting through absurdity with even more absurd certainty

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Stew has never woken anyone up, ever.

jokes.text: Oh, it worked! Here she comes!
jokes.setup: Charlie just said stew never wakes anyone up
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction as Dee wakes up
jokes.explanation: Simple ironic reversal but lacks originality

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, it worked! Here she comes!

jokes.text: It's, uh 'Patty's' with a T. P-A-T-T-Y.
jokes.setup: Dee thinks they're back at Paddy's Pub
jokes.punchline: They're at a knockoff Irish pub with slightly different spelling
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that establishes their predicament

dialogue.line: It's, uh 'Patty's' with a T. P-A-T-T-Y.

jokes.text: You can smell fresh peat grass burning in the fireplace...along with the evidence of Frank's involvement in a sex trafficking ring. Alleged.
jokes.setup: Dennis describing the charming pub atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of sex trafficking evidence mixed with tourist pleasantries
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sunny escalation - romantic atmosphere undercut by horrific implication delivered casually. Frank's 'alleged' makes it even funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You can smell fresh peat grass burning in the fireplace...along with the evidence of Frank's involvement in a sex trafficking ring. Alleged.

jokes.text: No, because we don't have COVID like you do. You're sick.
jokes.setup: Dennis complaining about sheep wool bothering him
jokes.punchline: Mac bluntly stating Dennis has COVID
jokes.explanation: Typical Mac bluntness, sets up Dennis's denial subplot

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, because we don't have COVID like you do. You're sick.

jokes.text: H-He's the, uh... cheese mongrel. Wow. I did not know he was a mongrel. Um, how severe is it? Can-can he walk? Does he drool, or...
jokes.setup: Bartender says Shelley Kelly is the local cheesemonger
jokes.punchline: Charlie mishears as 'mongrel' and assumes it's a medical condition
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Charlie - mishearing a word and following the logic to its absurd conclusion with genuine concern

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: H-He's the, uh... cheese mongrel. Wow. I did not know he was a mongrel. Um, how severe is it? Can-can he walk? Does he drool, or...

jokes.text: Your brother, the cheese monster.
jokes.setup: Charlie's confusion about cheesemonger/mongrel
jokes.punchline: Mac further corrupts it to 'cheese monster'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Charlie's mishearing, creates even more confusion

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Your brother, the cheese monster.

jokes.text: You know, when I was Irish first, then I wanted to find out what my heritage was. But now that I'm gay first, and that's my identity, then maybe I should just be plowing a bunch of dudes, and maybe a bunch of cheese monsters.
jokes.setup: Mac's ongoing identity crisis between being Irish and gay
jokes.punchline: Wanting to have sex with cheese monsters as part of gay identity exploration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac - overthinking identity while casually suggesting sex with mythical cheese creatures

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You know, when I was Irish first, then I wanted to find out what my heritage was. But now that I'm gay first, and that's my identity, then maybe I should just be plowing a bunch of dudes, and maybe a bunch of cheese monsters.

jokes.text: Identity doesn't have to factor into absolutely every decision you make. If a man wants to stand, he doesn't go, 'Uh, uh, what would a Dutchman do? Would a Dutchman stand?' And if an Italian wants to eat a sandwich, he doesn't go, 'I have to eat plates of spaghetti because I'm an Italian.'
jokes.setup: Mac's constant identity crisis discussions
jokes.punchline: Dennis's absurd examples of overthinking ethnic identity in mundane situations
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dennis rant with specific, absurd examples that perfectly capture Mac's overthinking

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Identity doesn't have to factor into absolutely every decision you make. If a man wants to stand, he doesn't go, 'Uh, uh, what would a Dutchman do? Would a Dutchman stand?' And if an Italian wants to eat a sandwich, he doesn't go, 'I have to eat plates of spaghetti because I'm an Italian.'

jokes.text: If I got to stop mid-rant every time I want to order a beer, it's gonna interrupt my flow.
jokes.setup: Dennis giving a passionate speech while simultaneously ordering drinks
jokes.punchline: Dennis acknowledging his tendency to rant while being annoyed at interruptions
jokes.explanation: Self-aware Dennis moment acknowledging his own pompous behavior patterns

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: If I got to stop mid-rant every time I want to order a beer, it's gonna interrupt my flow.

jokes.text: I think what they say is, 'Top o' the mornin'.' Ah, that's a stereotype.
jokes.setup: Dee trying to use Irish phrases to seem authentic
jokes.punchline: Immediately called out for using stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Classic Dee cultural insensitivity immediately backfiring

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I think what they say is, 'Top o' the mornin'.'

dialogue.speaker: Castle Owner
dialogue.line: Ah, that's a stereotype.

jokes.text: [Dennis coughs uncontrollably during his attempt to do the talking]
jokes.setup: Dennis insisting he can control his cough and do the talking
jokes.punchline: Immediately coughs right when trying to speak
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical comedy timing - Dennis's arrogance immediately undermined

jokes.text: What‐what time is it? What day is it? Wait, did I miss the thing? What thing? Oh, the show? Uh, the acting thing? Oh, yeah, you missed it.
jokes.setup: Dee waking up confused about time
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing she missed her acting opportunity
jokes.explanation: Shows the gang's casual cruelty - they don't care that Dee missed her opportunity.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: What‐what time is it? What day is it? Wait, did I miss the thing?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What thing?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Oh, the show? Uh, the acting thing?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, yeah, you missed it.

jokes.text: You know what it is? He's a mongrel. And that's why I've always been the talker. We have to keep him in the basement in America, 'cause one of his ticks is that he brutally masturbates himself.
jokes.setup: Dee explaining Dennis's coughing to the castle owner
jokes.punchline: Dee invents increasingly horrific lies about Dennis being mentally disabled
jokes.explanation: Dee at her cruelest and most creative, escalating from 'mongrel' to disturbing sexual details

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You know what it is? He's a mongrel. And that's why I've always been the talker. We have to keep him in the basement in America, 'cause one of his ticks is that he brutally masturbates himself.

jokes.text: Yeah, he does a real number on it. Yeah. He's shredded it down to a little tiny nub at this point.
jokes.setup: Dee already describing Dennis's supposed masturbation problem
jokes.punchline: Graphic details about the physical damage
jokes.explanation: Dee doubling down with disturbing specificity while Dennis can only make muffled protests

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah, he does a real number on it. Yeah. He's shredded it down to a little tiny nub at this point.

jokes.text: Yeah. He‐he's shredded it down to a little tiny nub at this point.
jokes.setup: Dee's story about Dennis's masturbation problem
jokes.punchline: She adds disturbing anatomical details
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalation with graphic imagery. 'Little tiny nub' is both specific and horrifying.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah. He‐he's shredded it down to a little tiny nub at this point.

jokes.text: He's also allergic to sheep.
jokes.setup: Dee has already painted Dennis as severely disabled
jokes.punchline: Adding random sheep allergy as final insult
jokes.explanation: Good capper to Dee's lies, explains Dennis's earlier complaints

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: He's also allergic to sheep.

jokes.text: I bash a lot of rats in my business, too. And‐and rats love cheese, so I imagine y‐you get a lot of 'em here.
jokes.setup: Shelley was bashing rats when Charlie arrived
jokes.punchline: Charlie casually mentions he also bashes rats, treating violence as normal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - treating rat violence as a normal part of business networking. Very character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I bash a lot of rats in my business, too. And‐and rats love cheese, so I imagine y‐you get a lot of 'em here.

jokes.text: I bash a lot of rats in my business, too. And rats love cheese, so I imagine you get a lot of 'em here.
jokes.setup: Shelley apologizing for bashing rats
jokes.punchline: Charlie relating through his own rat-bashing experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie - finding common ground through pest control and making logical connections

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I bash a lot of rats in my business, too. And rats love cheese, so I imagine you get a lot of 'em here.

jokes.text: This is the tits right here... That's a miss. It's got something in it like sand. It's full of sand... It's seeds. And there's too many of 'em.
jokes.setup: Charlie enthusiastically trying cheese samples
jokes.punchline: Immediately regretting it and providing detailed texture analysis
jokes.explanation: Charlie's enthusiastic-to-disgusted arc with specific food criticism

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: This is the tits right here... That's a miss. It's got something in it like sand. It's full of sand... It's seeds. And there's too many of 'em.

jokes.text: Okay, so, I used to be Irish, and now I'm Dutch... I wanted to run through a bunch of pipe, if you know what I mean... But then I decided, you know what? I only want one man inside of me. The Big Man. The Big Man upstairs... I'll take all three of them at once. I've done that before. That was more in a physical sense, not a spiritual one.
jokes.setup: Mac confessing to a priest about his spiritual awakening
jokes.punchline: Inadvertently describing sexual acts while talking about religious devotion
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy - Mac's complete obliviousness to sexual innuendo while discussing the Holy Trinity. The casual 'I've done that before' is perfectly horrifying.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Okay, so, I used to be Irish, and now I'm Dutch... I wanted to run through a bunch of pipe, if you know what I mean... But then I decided, you know what? I only want one man inside of me. The Big Man. The Big Man upstairs... I'll take all three of them at once. I've done that before. That was more in a physical sense, not a spiritual one.

jokes.text: I've done that before. That was more in a physical sense, not a spiritual one. And believe me, it was messy. Totally worth it, though. I loved it.
jokes.setup: Mac continues the Trinity metaphor
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to threesome with graphic details
jokes.explanation: Good escalation but becomes more crude than clever. Still fits Mac's character.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I've done that before. That was more in a physical sense, not a spiritual one. And believe me, it was messy. Totally worth it, though. I loved it.

jokes.text: I'll just get in the back of the line, and then, uh, get the cracker, and then we'll talk later.
jokes.setup: Mac realizing he interrupted a religious service
jokes.punchline: Calling communion wafer a 'cracker' while trying to be respectful
jokes.explanation: Mac's attempt at religious respect undermined by casual terminology

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I'll just get in the back of the line, and then, uh, get the cracker, and then we'll talk later.

jokes.text: Yeah. A mother went insane and beheaded all four children. Just chopped their heads right off... that's decidedly less charming, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Dennis romanticizing the castle's dark history
jokes.punchline: Learning it was recent family murder, not historical
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of Dennis's romantic notions with modern horror. Dennis's understatement response is great.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah. A mother went insane and beheaded all four children. Just chopped their heads right off... that's decidedly less charming, isn't it?

jokes.text: I banged her, too. All right, well, Mom got around.
jokes.setup: Shelley telling Charlie he loved Charlie's mother
jokes.punchline: Frank casually mentioning he also had sex with her, Charlie's resigned acceptance
jokes.explanation: Frank's inappropriate timing and Charlie's deadpan acceptance of his mother's reputation

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I banged her, too.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: All right, well, Mom got around.

jokes.setup: Father-son bonding over Charlie's deceased mother
jokes.punchline: Frank admits to also sleeping with her, Charlie's casual acceptance
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect character reactions. Charlie's nonchalant response is perfectly in character.

dialogue.line: I banged her, too.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie

jokes.text: [Lengthy Irish folk song performance by Charlie and Shelley, Frank awkwardly trying to join in]
jokes.setup: Father-son bonding moment through song

jokes.explanation: Sustained cringe sequence with Frank's 'And I am Frank, and I factor in somehow' being particularly pathetic

jokes.text: I've been S'ing and F'ing my way through life for far too long. I think it's time that I started sucking down the words of the Bible. And instead of looking for pieces of ass, I'd just look for peace.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to express spiritual transformation
jokes.punchline: Sexual wordplay while attempting to sound religious
jokes.explanation: Mac's inability to discuss anything without sexual undertones, even when genuinely trying to be spiritual

jokes.punchline: He euphemizes swearing then uses 'sucking down' for studying

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I just need a good night's sleep.

jokes.text: I'm not even talking to you. I'm talking to the castle.
jokes.setup: Dee asking who Dennis is talking to

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: The hair is a lie, and therefore, it's better to just remove the head altogether.

jokes.text: The hair is a lie, and therefore, it's better to just remove the head altogether.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
